findings.title: Hardcoded secret key
findings.description: The secret key used for encoding the token payload is hardcoded. This is a significant security risk as it can be easily accessed by an attacker.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a secure method to store and retrieve the secret key, such as an environment variable or a secure key management system.

findings.title: Insecure password storage
findings.description: The passwords are stored in plaintext in the users_db dictionary. This is a significant security risk as it can be easily accessed by an attacker.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a secure password hashing algorithm like bcrypt, scrypt, or PBKDF2 to store passwords securely.

findings.title: Missing input validation
findings.description: The login function does not validate the input username and password. This can lead to potential security vulnerabilities.
findings.suggested_fix: Add input validation to ensure that the username and password are not empty and meet the required criteria.

findings.title: Insecure token expiration
findings.description: The token expiration time is set to 15 minutes by default. This can be a security risk if the token is not properly validated.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a secure method to set the token expiration time, such as a configurable variable or a secure token expiration mechanism.

findings.title: Test user creation
findings.description: A test user is created in the startup event. This can be a security risk if the test user is not properly removed in a production environment.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the test user creation in a production environment or use a secure method to create test users.
